title: शनि चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
जय गणेश गिरिजा सुवन, मंगल मूल सुजान।
कहत अयोध्यादास तुम, देउ अभय वरदान॥

जय जय श्री शनिदेव प्रभु, सुनहु विनय महाराज।
करहु कृपा हे रवि तनय, राखहु जन की लाज॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जयति जयति शनिदेव दयाला।
करत सदा भक्तन प्रतिपाला॥
चार भुजा तनु श्याम विराजे।
माथे रतन मुकुट छबि छाजे॥
परम विशाल मनोहर भाला।
नील वस्त्र शिर सुन्दर माला॥
कर में गदा त्रिशूल कुठारा।
पल पल करत सृष्टि संहारा॥
कर में शोभित नीलम प्यारा।
भक्तन को वर देत अपारा॥
गिद्ध सवारी करत निराला।
शनि देव करत प्रतिपाला॥
सूर्यपुत्र छाया महतारी।
यम भ्राता करते हितकारी॥
दक्षिण दिशा के तुम ही स्वामी।
शनि देव तुम परम अंतर्यामी॥
बीस हजार योजन ऊपर जाना।
बैठ सिंहासन करत विधाना॥
तीनों लोक में डंका बाजे।
हैं सब ग्रह शनि के ही काजे॥
शनि बिना सब सून हैं सूना।
करत सदा रण में पैने लूना॥
सूर्य चन्द्र राहू ग्रह आवें।
शुक्र बुध तव चरण मनावें॥
जग में सकल किया तैं साजा।
भुक्ति मुक्ति सब तेरो काजा॥
जो जन तुमको ध्यान लगावें।
सो सात जन्म का दुख मिट जावे॥
जो पण्डित सुयोग्य बुलावे।
विधिवत पूजा मन्त्र चलावे॥
तिल तेल उड़द चढ़ावे जेई।
सो भक्त सदा सुखी रहे तेई॥
पिपरा मूल सरसों तेला।
शनि देव शनिवार को मेला॥
शनि ग्रह के जब होय बड़ाई।
तब दशरथ ने की विनवाई॥
राजा विक्रमादित्य सताये।
शनि की महिमा जग में गाये॥
हरिश्चन्द्र पर शनि भारी।
तब प्रभु राजा त्यागे नारी॥
कर्ण शनि ग्रसे अपना।
भयो युद्ध में रणछोड़ सपना॥
रावण ऊपर शनि जब आये।
लंका राज विभीषण पाये॥
पाण्डव ऊपर शनि जब छाये।
बन बन भटकत कष्ट उठाये॥
शनि वचन सत्य ना टारे।
हरि हर ब्रह्मा सब ही हारे॥
जो जन शनिवार व्रत करई।
भक्तिभाव से शनि को भजई॥
काल दोष रवि नन्दन हारे।
ग्रह बाधा नश मंगल द्वारे॥
शनि चालीसा जो नर गावे।
घर बैठे नर मनवांछित फल पावे॥
कर जोरि विनय करे जो कोई।
शनि दोष तासु निकट न होई॥
तुलसीदास सदा शनि चेरा।
कीजै नाथ हृदय महँ डेरा॥

॥ दोहा ॥
पाठ शनि चालीसा को, भक्त करें धर ध्यान।
कष्ट मिटे सब जन के, होत सदा कल्यान॥

॥ इति श्री शनि चालीसा सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Śani Chālīsā ||

|| Dohā ||
Jaya Gaṇeśa Girijā suvana, maṅgala mūla sujāna.
Kahata Ayodhyādāsa tuma, deu abhaya varadāna.

Jaya jaya Shrī Śanideva Prabhu, sunahu vinaya Mahārāja.
Karahu kṛpā he Ravi tanaya, rākhahu jana kī lāja.

|| Chaupāī ||
Jayati jayati Śanideva dayālā.
Karata sadā bhaktana pratipālā.
Chāra bhujā tanu śyāma virāje.
Māthe ratana mukuṭa chhabi chhāje.
Parama viśāla manohara bhālā.
Nīla vastra śira sundara mālā.
Kara meṁ gadā triśūla kuṭhārā.
Pala pala karata sṛṣṭi saṁhārā.
Kara meṁ śobhita nīlama pyārā.
Bhaktana ko vara deta apārā.
Giddha savārī karata nirālā.
Śani deva karata pratipālā.
Sūryaputra Chhāyā mahatārī.
Yama bhrātā karate hitakārī.
Dakṣiṇa diśā ke tumhī svāmī.
Śani deva tuma parama antaryāmī.
Bīsa hajāra yojana ūpara jānā.
Baiṭha siṁhāsana karata vidhānā.
Tīnoṁ loka meṁ ḍaṅkā bāje.
Haiṁ saba graha Śani ke hī kāje.
Śani binā saba sūna haiṁ sūnā.
Karata sadā raṇa meṁ paine lūnā.
Sūrya Chandra Rāhu graha āveṁ.
Śukra Budha tava charaṇa manāveṁ.
Jaga meṁ sakala kiyā taiṁ sājā.
Bhukti mukti saba tero kājā.
Jo jana tumako dhyāna lagāveṁ.
So sāta janma kā dukha miṭa jāve.
Jo paṇḍita suyogya bulāve.
Vidhivata pūjā mantra chalāve.
Tila tela uṛada chaṛhāve jeī.
So bhakta sadā sukhī rahe teī.
Piparā mūla sarasoṁ telā.
Śani deva Śanivāra ko melā.
Śani graha ke jaba hoya baṛāī.
Taba Daśaratha ne kī vinavāī.
Rājā Vikramāditya satāye.
Śani kī mahimā jaga meṁ gāye.
Hariśchandra para Śani bhārī.
Taba Prabhu rājā tyāge nārī.
Karṇa Śani grase apanā.
Bhayo yuddha meṁ raṇachhoṛa sapanā.
Rāvaṇa ūpara Śani jaba āye.
Laṅkā rāja Vibhīṣaṇa pāye.
Pāṇḍava ūpara Śani jaba chhāye.
Bana bana bhaṭakata kaṣṭa uṭhāye.
Śani vachana satya nā ṭāre.
Hari Hara Brahmā saba hī hāre.
Jo jana Śanivāra vrata karaī.
Bhaktibhāva se Śani ko bhajaī.
Kāla doṣa Ravi nandana hāre.
Graha bādhā naśa maṅgala dvāre.
Śani Chālīsā jo nara gāve.
Ghara baiṭhe nara manavāñchhita phala pāve.
Kara joṛi vinaya kare jo koī.
Śani doṣa tāsu nikaṭa na hoī.
Tulasīdāsa sadā Śani cherā.
Kījai nātha hṛdaya mahaṁ ḍerā.

|| Dohā ||
Pāṭha Śani Chālīsā ko, bhakta kareṁ dhara dhyāna.
Kaṣṭa miṭe saba jana ke, hota sadā kalyāna.

|| Iti Shrī Śani Chālīsā sampūrṇa ||
hindiMeaning: गिरिजा पुत्र गणेश जी की जय हो, जो मंगल के मूल हैं। अयोध्यादास कहते हैं कि आप अभय वरदान दीजिए। श्री शनिदेव प्रभु की जय हो, महाराज मेरी विनती सुनिए। हे रवि पुत्र! कृपा कीजिए और अपने भक्तों की लाज रखिए। दयालु शनिदेव की जय हो जो सदा भक्तों की रक्षा करते हैं। श्याम शरीर चार भुजाओं वाले, मस्तक पर रत्न जड़ित मुकुट शोभित है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of the malefic effects of shani sade sati and shani dhaiya
Traditionally invoked for protection from planetary afflictions of saturn
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles and suffering caused by karmic debts
Invokes the blessing of prosperity and removes poverty
Invokes fulfillment of desires when recited on saturdays
Traditionally invoked for protection from untimely death and accidents
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Shani Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as शनि चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Shani Dev. Rooted in Traditional Hindu Prayer, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to grant fulfillment of desires when recited on Saturdays and protect from planetary afflictions of Saturn. It is also said to bestow prosperity and remove poverty. This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) to invoke divine grace. Comprising 40 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shani Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Powerful prayer to Saturn God for Sade Sati remedy and planetary protection.
relatedSearches: Shanidev mustard oil black sesame offering

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shani called the 'Karmaphal Daata' in the Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Chalisa repeatedly addresses Shanidev as Karmaphal Daata — 'the dispenser of the fruit of actions.' Hinduism Facts explains that 'in Hindu astrology and religious books, Lord Shani is regarded as the Karmaphal Daata, the dispenser of deeds (actions), who grants results based on one's past and present actions.' Unlike other planetary deities who can be appeased to alter karma, Shani is the cosmic auditor who delivers exactly what one has earned. The Chalisa therefore frames worship not as bribery but as conscious acceptance of karmic law, with Shani's grace softening the form (not the fact) of the result. (Source: Hinduism Facts Shani Chalisa; Mantra.tips Shani Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Shani Chalisa specifically used to remedy Sade Sati?
seoFaqs.answer: Sade Sati is the seven-and-a-half-year period when Saturn transits the twelfth, first, and second houses from one's natal Moon — considered one of the most challenging astrological phases in Vedic Jyotish. Mantra.tips notes that 'reciting the Shani Chalisa lyrics daily is considered one of the most effective remedies to mitigate the hardships caused by the planet Saturn. It is believed to drastically reduce the suffering caused during the 7.5-year (Sade Sati) and 2.5-year (Dhaiya) cycles of Saturn.' Pandits typically prescribe daily Saturday recitation throughout the entire Sade Sati period, often combined with Hanuman Chalisa (since Hanuman is said to control Shani). (Source: Mantra.tips Shani Chalisa; Bhagwan App Shani Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does the Chalisa itself recommend a 40-day anushthana?
seoFaqs.answer: Hinduism Facts and Bhagwan App both note that 'the Chalisa itself suggests reciting it continuously for 40 days for significant results.' This 40-day vrata (mandala-paath) is rooted in the same logic as other Chalisa anushthanas: 40 days completes one mandala (cycle) sufficient for embedded mental impressions (samskaras) to take root. The vrata is begun on a Saturday — preferably Shani Amavasya, Shani Jayanti, or the first Saturday after a major planetary transit. Devotees maintain austerities (light food, no oil massage, no alcohol, charity to the poor and crows) for the full 40 days. (Source: Hinduism Facts Shani Chalisa; Mantra.tips 40-day prescription.)

seoFaqs.question: What stories about Shani's power over the gods does the Chalisa mention?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chalisa narrates how Shani's drishti (planetary glance) afflicted even the greatest figures of mythology: Sri Rama lost his kingdom and went into 14-year exile under Shani's influence, Ravana lost Lanka, Harishchandra lost his throne and family, King Vikramaditya lost his kingdom and worked as a labourer, and Nala (of Nala-Damayanti) was separated from his wife. These narrations serve a pedagogical purpose: even avatars and ideal kings could not bypass karma, so ordinary devotees should accept Shani's discipline with humility rather than resentment. The Chalisa concludes that those who worship Shani sincerely turn the experience into spiritual growth. (Source: Hinduism Facts Shani Chalisa; HindUtsav Shani Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: What offerings are traditionally made alongside the Shani Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Shani offerings include: black sesame seeds (kala til), mustard oil (sarson ka tel) poured on Shani-linga or murti, blue/black flowers especially neelakamal, urad dal (black gram), iron objects (a horseshoe is classic), and food given to crows (Shani's vahana) and to the poor. The most famous Shani temple, Shani Shingnapur in Ahmednagar district of Maharashtra, where the deity is a self-manifested swayambhu black stone with no roof, attracts massive Saturday crowds who recite the Chalisa while pouring oil. The temple village famously has no doors on any house, by Shani's protection. (Source: Devshoppe Shani Chalisa; Bhagwan App Shanidev.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shani considered both feared and revered as a teacher?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Chalisa balances two faces of Shani: the dreaded judge whose seven-and-a-half-year transit upends lives, and the compassionate teacher whose hardships forge wisdom. Mantra.tips captures this paradox: 'Chanting Shani Chalisa mitigates the malefic effects of Saturn in one's horoscope, reduces the impact of Sade Sati and Shani Dasha, and transforms punishment into learning experiences.' Scholars trace this dual nature to Shani's Puranic biography: son of Surya and Chhaya, born blue-black from Chhaya's penance, lame from a curse, and granted by Shiva the role of dharma-administrator. He punishes only because he loves dharma, not because he hates jivas. (Source: Mantra.tips Shani Chalisa; World Authors Shani Chalisa.)
